preamble: The phonetic transformation from the diphthong /ue/ to the monophthong /o/ involves the simplification of a rising diphthong into a single, close-mid back rounded vowel. This process, known as monophthongization, often occurs in unstressed or weakly stressed syllables, or in specific lexical items, and is characteristic of colloquial or dialectal speech.

examples.note: Colloquial reduction of the diphthong /ue/ to /o/.
Colloquial reduction of the diphthong /ue/ to /o/.
Levelling of the diphthong [ue] to [o] in colloquial speech.

sources: López Villaseñor, M. (2023). Variations of Vowel Sequences in the Speech of Granada: About Muy and Pues. Sociolinguistic Studies, 17(1), 1–22.
Monroy, R. (2015). Murcian Spanish. Journal of the International Phonetic Association, 45(2), 229-238.
